title: Test a Component or Application Shell
breadcrumbs: Test a Component or Application Shell
text: Test a Component or Application Shell You can test an individual presentation component from the Component Editor of the IDE. For form components and desktop application shells, you can also use the /tst switch in the Uniface IDE command line. This spawns a test mode environment appropriate to the component. By default, dynamic and static server pages are opened in the default web browser; other components are run in an IDE process. However, you cam configure the start-up parameters for test mode using assignment settings. For more information, see Configure Component Testing for the Uniface IDE . Any DBMS that you are using is accessed in the same way as in a production environment. Data can be retrieved, stored, and erased. To run services and server application shells in test mode, use the Uniface Debugger, for example, using the /tst and /deb switches. For more information, see Uniface Debugger . To test a component from the Component Editor, open the component and choose Compile & Test or Actions > Test . This starts the component and activates its exec operation. To run a form component from the command line, start the Uniface IDE and enter /tst FormName in the command line dialog. To test an application shell, start the Uniface IDE and enter /tst ApsName .aps . To test a dynamic server page on a mobile device, choose Actions >Show QR Code and read the QR code with your mobile device. For more information, see Testing DSPs on Mobile Devices . Test Mode Restrictions When testing components other than dynamic and static server pages, be aware of the following: The component or application runs in a Uniface IDE process, rather than in your own application, so it maintains the behavior of the IDE. For example, assignment settings such as $MESSAGE_LINE and $MENU_BAR do not affect the component. Uniface uses the values for $variation and $language as defined in the project default preferences for library and language. For more information, see $variation and language . Uniface clears the general variables $1 through $99 before activating the exec operation of a component. If the component cannot run correctly without these values, run the component in debug mode. If a path to a DBMS or network is needed, you are prompted for the path. In addition, problems can occur when the Repository is using the same DBMS as the entities in the component. The Repository entities should be kept separate from user data. If the apexit statement is used in any ProcScript while testing a component, control is returned to the operating system. For more information, see apexit . Related Links /tst Run a component or application shell in test mode. Was this topic helpful? Like Dislike
